title: Dicranopteris quadripartita
taxonomy: Dicranopteris quadripartita is a species of fern in the family Matoniaceae. It is a type of vascular plant and a member of the phylum Pteridophyta. The classification of Dicranopteris quadripartita is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Gleicheniales, Family: Matoniaceae. Dicranopteris quadripartita is a cosmopolitan species, found in many regions around the world. This species is often classified as a nothogenic fern due to its unique characteristics.
uses: Dicranopteris quadripartita, also known as the ferny grass or umbrella fern, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. In some Asian countries, it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is also used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. In addition, Dicranopteris quadripartita is used to treat skin conditions, including eczema and acne, due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Dicranopteris quadripartita for medicinal purposes.

intro: Dicranopteris quadripartita, also known as the forked fern or shui buto, is a type of fern native to tropical regions, characterized by its distinctive forked or divided fronds. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, treating fever and respiratory infections, and possessing antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dicranopteris quadripartita include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Dicranopteris quadripartita is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, or used in combination with other herbs in various formulations to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
